title: Rich Crispy Light Croatian Duck Stir-fry
description: A rich and crispy light Croatian dish featuring duck sauce, temper, and a low-carb twist for a flavorful meal.
instructions: Season the duck breast fillets with salt and pepper. Sauté them in a bit of olive oil until golden brown on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove from heat and set aside to keep warm while you prepare the sauce.
In a small saucepan, add chicken broth concentrate, apple cider vinegar, and a pinch of salt. Heat gently over low flame for about 5 minutes until reduced slightly.
Meanwhile, in another pan, sauté diced onions with minced garlic until translucent. Add sliced bell peppers, zucchini, and cumin seeds to the pan. Stir well and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Pour the sauce into the pan with vegetables and let it simmer for about 5 more minutes to allow flavors to meld together. Adjust seasoning if needed.
Return the duck fillets to the pan and stir gently to coat them evenly with the sauce. Cook for another minute until all the ingredients are heated through.
Transfer to a serving dish, garnish with chopped cilantro (if using), and serve hot as a main course.
